habitat: Freshwater lakes; pools; marshes; bogs; swamps; coastal areas; and wetlands
story: An icon of the skies, the Whooper Swan (Cygnus cygnus) is a species from the Anatidae family. It thrives in Freshwater lakes; pools; marshes; bogs; swamps; coastal areas; and wetlands, where it interacts with its environment in unique ways. With a conservation status of Least Concern (LC), the Whooper Swan is a focus for conservationists worldwide. Understanding the needs of the Whooper Swan helps us better comprehend the challenges facing our planet's wildlife. This poster is a celebration of the Whooper Swan, a bird that inspires wonder and highlights the critical importance of conservation efforts.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
